mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month change exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 with S&P 500 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Headline CPI inflation exceeding 4 percent year-over-year signals an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2021 CPI vintage implies YoY inflation above 5%
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms a broad-based demand recovery that lifts corporate earnings expectations beyond what either indicator predicts alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint movement of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce a 60-day forward return Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns when both rise are not higher than when one rises.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, even a marginal increase in the effective federal funds rate signals the start of a hiking cycle, sharply raising discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Effective Fed Funds at 0.09% in August 2021 vintage
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, month-over-month increases do not correspond to negative 20-day forward returns (average return > 0% or hit rate > 50%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and operating margin compression, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the Rank IC is not negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts sustained above demographic replacement levels drive construction employment and durable goods consumption, generating cyclical earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST level changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 with S&P 500 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over two months triggers recessionary expectations, reducing risk appetite and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 2 months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and a declining federal funds rate indicates a recessionary easing environment where monetary stimulus fails to offset earnings destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases over 3 months while FEDFUNDS decreases over the same period
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS does not produce negative average 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term Rank IC is above zero over a 3-year window.
